contents:
# China upgrades Ream Base in Cambodia for PLA Navy power projection in South China Sea

## Overview
China funded significant upgrades to Ream Base since 2019, granting long-term access to People's Liberation Army Navy. The base strengthens China's ability to project power in Gulf of Thailand and across South China Sea. This raises regional security concerns amid U.S.-led freedom of navigation operations.[1]

## Details
Upgrades central to China-Vietnam maritime tensions. Expert Mayfield from Institute for Study of Diplomacy noted base's role in power projection. Enhances PLA Navy operations in contested waters. Part of broader South China Sea disputes with Vietnam. Historical grievances fuel ongoing conflicts. Base located in Sihanoukville, key to regional dynamics.[1]

**Location**: Ream Naval Base, Sihanoukville, Cambodia

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @XHNews, which covers Xinhua news China official, PLA exercises, Taiwan, South China Sea. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following Xinhua news China official will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
